2015-05-21.abstract: President Ramzan A. Kadyrov of Chechnya finally had enough Wednesday of social media users mocking him relentlessly for seeming to push polygamy. His solution? Keep women locked up at home.

Ethiopia’s Ruling
Party Is Expected to
Keep Grip on Power

2015-05-25.abstract: With the number of overseas tourists from China surpassing 100 million, and many of those descending en masse in large tour groups, China is trying to ensure that they don’t abuse their welcome.

2015-05-25.title: Malaysian Police Suspect That Mass Graves Are Those of Migrants
2015-05-25.abstract: More than 139 graves, some containing multiple bodies, have been found in 28 camps near the Thai border in the state of Perlis, a police official said.

2015-05-25.title: Malaysia Police Chief Comments on Graves
2015-05-25.abstract: Malaysia’s police chief says 139 graves believed to contain the remains of migrants have been found near the country’s border with Thailand.

2015-05-26.title: After a Year of Outsize Expectations, Narendra Modi Adjusts His Plan for India
2015-05-26.abstract: The Indian prime minister gave a speech on Monday that was notable for the subjects it avoided: Large-scale job creation, manufacturing and urbanization.

2015-05-26.title: Fire Kills 38 at Seniors Home in China
2015-05-26.abstract: With a rapidly aging population and an underfunded social security system, China faces increasing pressure to provide safe, affordable care for retirees.

2015-05-26.title: Jason Rezaian of Washington Post Goes on Trial in Iran
2015-05-26.abstract: The trial, which is not open to the public, began in a Tehran courtroom, the semiofficial Tasnim news agency reported. The charges include espionage.

2015-05-26.title: U.S. Caution in Strikes Gives ISIS an Edge, Many Iraqis Say
2015-05-26.abstract: Many Iraqi commanders and some American officers say that exercising caution with airstrikes has harmed the coalition’s larger effort to destroy the Islamic State.

2015-05-26.title: Chinese Distiller Sued in Test of State v. Private Ownership
2015-05-26.abstract: A family has sued the liquor maker Wuliangye over ownership of pits used to make baijiu, a powerful, clear liquor.

2015-05-27.title: Sephardic Jews Feel Bigotry’s Sting in Turkey and a Pull Back to Spain
2015-05-27.abstract: Thousands of Sephardic Jews in Turkey who trace their ancestry to Spain are applying for Spanish citizenship in anticipation of a bill granting nationality to Jews expelled during the Inquisition.

2015-05-27.title: Libyan Prime Minister Survives Assassination Attempt Amid Protests
2015-05-27.abstract: Abdullah al-Thinni had faced hostile questioning inside Parliament, and demands for his resignation from armed demonstrators outside.

2015-05-27.title: Mexico: Michoacán Killings Prompt Human Rights Inquiry
2015-05-27.abstract: The National Human Rights Commission has opened an investigation into the killing of 42 people last week in a confrontation with the federal police.

2015-05-27.title: Jason Rezaian Trial in Iran May Be More About Leverage Than Justice
2015-05-27.abstract: The fates of Americans imprisoned in Iran have often centered on how the country’s negotiations with the United States are going.

2015-05-27.title: Most Hong Kongers Support Banning Ivory Sales, Poll Finds
2015-05-27.abstract: A survey found that ivory was unpopular among residents in Hong Kong, despite the city being a major market for ivory, much of it illegal.

2015-05-28.title: Al Qaeda’s Branch in Syria Denies Planning Attacks Abroad
2015-05-28.abstract: The leader of the Nusra Front said in a television interview that Al Qaeda had ordered it to keep its focus on toppling President Bashar al-Assad.

2015-05-28.title: A Region’s Soccer Strongmen Are Facing a Hard Fall
2015-05-28.abstract: A case in which 14 senior sports officials and executives across the Americas have been charged offers a glimpse into the sort of deals that have long come under fire but have rarely led to a criminal proceeding.

2015-05-28.title: Obama’s Chief Negotiator in Iran Nuclear Talks Plans to Depart After Deadline for Deal
2015-05-28.abstract: With the departure of Wendy R. Sherman, all the top negotiators on Iran since 2013 will have left the administration, leaving questions about who will help carry out a deal if one is struck.

2015-05-28.title: Medical Need Climbs Alongside Death Toll in Yemen
2015-05-28.abstract: Some 8.6 million people, or about a third of the country’s population, were in “urgent need of medical help,” the World Health Organization warned.

2015-05-28.title: For Turkey’s Ruler, Criticism From New York Is Not Fit to Print
2015-05-28.abstract: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan seems to have assumed a new, unofficial role this week as the country’s chief news media critic.

2015-05-29.title: ISIS Alternates Stick and Carrot to Control Palmyra
2015-05-29.abstract: The militants have alternated between terrorizing residents and courting them in their takeover of the ancient Syrian city.

2015-05-29.title: U.N. Resolves to Combat Plundering of Antiquities by ISIS
2015-05-29.abstract: A resolution adopted unanimously by the General Assembly amounted to the broadest international condemnation so far to the cultural destruction wrought by the Islamic State.

2015-05-29.title: Yemen: Amnesty Says Antiaircraft Fire by Houthis Has Killed Many Civilians
2015-05-29.abstract: Antiaircraft munitions fired by Yemen’s Houthi rebels have killed or maimed scores of civilians in Sana.

2015-05-29.title: Syria: Insurgent Coalition Pushes Assad’s Forces From a Stronghold
2015-05-29.abstract: Insurgents seized one of the last Syrian government positions in the northern province of Idlib on Thursday night.

2015-05-29.title: U.S. Signals Willingness to Expand Military Cooperation With Nigeria
2015-05-29.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry, in Abuja for the inauguration of Muhammadu Buhari, discussed cooperation against Boko Haram with the new president.

2015-05-30.title: Iraq: 14 Are Killed in Hotel Bombings
2015-05-30.abstract: The Islamic State took responsibility on Friday for car bombings that struck two luxury hotels in Baghdad, which a security official said had killed 14 people.

2015-05-30.title: Iran: Prisoners’ Relatives to Testify
2015-05-30.abstract: Relatives of three Americans imprisoned in Iran and of a fourth who has been missing there for eight years will be the featured witnesses on Tuesday at a House Foreign Affairs Committee hearing.

2015-05-30.title: Chinese Security Laws Elevate the Party and Stifle Dissent. Mao Would Approve.
2015-05-30.abstract: The new law, released in draft form this month, says security must be maintained “to realize the great rejuvenation of the Chinese nation.”

2015-05-30.title: U.N. Role Is Questioned in Sexual Abuse Inquiry
2015-05-30.abstract: An advocacy group leaks documents that it says showed the United Nations was slow to act on accusations that French soldiers were abusing boys in the Central African Republic.

2015-05-30.title: Vladimir Katriuk, Beekeeper Accused of Nazi War Crimes, Is Dead at 93
2015-05-30.abstract: Mr. Katriuk, No. 2 on a list of most wanted Nazi war criminals, had been at the center of a dispute between Russia and Canada over an extradition demand.

2015-05-31.title: Building of Islands Is Debated, but China and U.S. Skirt Conflict at Talks
2015-05-31.abstract: After a week of public rancor over China’s rush to build artificial islands on reefs, rocks and atolls in the disputed waters of the South China Sea, an annual security meeting in Singapore presented a chance to lower the temperature.

2015-05-31.title: Malaysians Seek Escape in Pulp Fiction as Government’s Grip Tightens
2015-05-31.abstract: A new-style pulp fiction in Malaysia has tapped into a desire for escapism among younger Malaysians as their country has become more socially conservative.

2015-05-31.title: Ex-President of Georgia to Lead Ukraine Region
2015-05-31.abstract: President Petro O. Poroshenko of Ukraine appointed Mikheil Saakashvili as governor of Odessa, turning to a longtime nemesis of President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia for help governing an area coveted by pro-Russian separatists.

2015-05-31.title: Russia’s Barring of Officials Seen as Reprisal
2015-05-31.abstract: The European Union confirmed the existence of a list of officials that Russia has barred, a move thought to be retaliation for the group’s barring of Russians said to have backed the annexation of Crimea.

2015-05-31.title: John Kerry Cuts Europe Trip Short After Breaking Leg in Bicycle Accident
2015-05-31.abstract: The secretary of state struck a curb while he was cycling near Scionzier, France, and was returning to Boston for treatment.
